run_condition_parity._note: Honest status of each run condition.

matched.commit: Same commit for both systems (run condition 1)
matched.working_tree: Clean and identical — all artifacts staged outside repo until benchmark complete (run condition 2)
matched.prompt: Same corpus prompt with scope instructions prepended (run condition 3)
matched.posture: Both systems accept -p flag for corpus posture (run condition 4, resolved at 52a968e9/e2057f2b)
matched.turn_budget: Both systems accept -n flag for corpus turn budget (run condition 4, resolved at 52a968e9/e2057f2b)
matched.codex_model: Same Codex model for both systems (run condition 5, matchable)
matched.reasoning_effort: Same reasoning effort setting (run condition 5, matchable)
matched.dialogue_timeout: 1200s for Codex API calls in both systems (run condition 5, matchable)

prompt_only._note: These controls are communicated via prompt instructions and enforced by post-hoc transcript review. Not mechanically enforced.

allowed_roots.contract_requirement: Run conditions 10-11: allowed_roots recorded and equivalent for both systems
allowed_roots.baseline: scope_envelope available in delegation envelope for codex-dialogue agent
allowed_roots.candidate: Gatherer agents support scope_envelope but the slash skill does not pass it; orchestrator containment guard constrains scouting paths but is not the same as scope_envelope
allowed_roots.enforcement: Prompt-level scope instructions plus post-hoc transcript review per contract v1 procedural enforcement
allowed_roots.resolution_to_mechanical: Update codex-collaboration dialogue skill to pass scope_envelope to gatherers

baseline_max_evidence.contract_requirement: Evidence budget section: baseline_max_evidence = 5, fixed for the comparison
baseline_max_evidence.enforcement: Prompt-level instructions plus post-hoc transcript review; overflow invalidates the run
baseline_max_evidence.candidate_max_evidence_note: Candidate MAX_EVIDENCE = 15 is natively enforced by orchestrator

max_evidence_values.note: Deliberate asymmetry per contract evidence budget section

evidence_path.candidate: Claude-side scouting via Glob/Grep/Read in gatherer and orchestrator agents
evidence_path.note: This is the variable under test

B1.prompt: Is the codex-collaboration MCP tool surface aligned with the normative spec, and what tools are still missing for full completion?

B3.prompt: Review the current context assembly redaction implementation for remaining coverage gaps or false-positive risks that still matter for Codex prompt safety.

B5.prompt: Is the advisory runtime rotation model specified strongly enough for privilege widening and narrowing, or where are the weak points?

B8.prompt: Can Claude-side scouting replace cross-model context-injection for dialogue in this repo, or what concrete quality loss would remain?
B8.allowed_roots_note: B8 uses anchored decomposition. allowed_roots is the flat union for transcript review; allowed_roots_groups preserves the per-group structure for adjudication. Each scouting step must stay within one group. Cross-group reasoning is expected; cross-group target expansion is not.
